matches.name: Tom Faulkner
matches.summary: Tom Faulkner is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group. They have uncovered fundamental connections between bounds on the processing of quantum information and the dynamics of quantum gravity and QFT.

matches.name: Tony Leggett
matches.summary: Tony Leggett is a Professor Emeritus in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group.

matches.name: Nancy Makri
research_areas: Theoretical quantum dynamics of large molecules and condensed phase systems
matches.summary: Nancy Makri is a Professor in the Chemistry Department. Their work is connected to Theoretical quantum dynamics of large molecules and condensed phase systems, Charge transfer reactions, and Quantum control of nanostructures. Nancy Makri's group strives to advance theoretical understanding of quantum mechanical processes in large molecules and the condensed phase. Full solution of the Schrödinger equation is feasible only for small molecules, as the required numerical effort increases exponentially with the number of particles.
